understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to maneuver selectable pieces (P and S) onto goal positions (+) within a limited step budget, likely by pushing blocks (B) or navigating around walls (#) on a pixel-based grid.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions (MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN) shift the selected piece but can be blocked by walls, boundaries, or objects; pushable blocks (B) can be shoved if adjacent in the movement direction. CLICK switches control to another selectable piece at the exact target_position coordinates (3 pixels per cell), consuming a step without moving objects physically. Walls (#) block movement variably, and heuristics like movement.blocked provide hints but aren't definitive—experimentation reveals behaviors. Repeated CLICKing between pieces wastes steps without progress.
